title: Single Responsibility Principle
signals: Class handles validation + persistence + external API calls
Controller methods exceed 80 lines or contain multiple distinct concerns
recommended_pattern: Extract FormRequest for validation
Extract Service/Action for business logic
Extract repository/client for external integrations

examples.bad: Controller validates, writes to DB, calls external API, transforms response
examples.good: Controller delegates to service; request handles validation; service returns result DTO

reference.topic: Single responsibility principle

title: Methods should do one thing
signals: Deep nesting, multiple loops/branches, and multiple unrelated side effects in one method
Method exceeds 40 lines without clear decomposition
recommended_pattern: Extract private methods with meaningful names
Extract small domain services/actions
Use early returns to reduce nesting

reference.topic: Methods should do just one thing

title: Skinny controllers; move logic out
signals: Controller builds complex queries and performs business decisions
Controller transforms models into arrays manually (instead of resources/DTOs)
recommended_pattern: Move query composition into Eloquent scopes or dedicated query objects
Move business decisions into service/action classes
Use API resources for response transformation

reference.topic: Fat models, skinny controllers

title: Validation belongs in FormRequest classes
signals: Controller uses $request->validate(...)
Validation arrays are defined inline in controllers
recommended_pattern: Create a FormRequest with rules() and authorize()
Typehint the FormRequest in the controller action
Use validated() data and/or DTO mapping

reference.topic: Validation

title: Business logic should live in service/action classes
signals: Business rules live inside controllers/models in hard-to-test ways
Multiple steps (pricing, eligibility, side effects) in one layer
recommended_pattern: Create a service/action class with a single public method (handle/execute)
Inject dependencies via constructor
Return a result object/DTO (or domain model) rather than arrays

reference.topic: Business logic should be in service class

title: Don't repeat yourself (DRY)
signals: Duplicate query fragments across multiple controllers/services
Same validation or mapping logic repeated across endpoints
recommended_pattern: Use Eloquent scopes for reusable query logic
Extract mapping into resources/DTOs
Create reusable services or helpers for repeated workflows

reference.topic: Don't repeat yourself

title: Prefer Eloquent + Collections when appropriate
signals: Raw SQL/Query Builder used for routine CRUD without a strong reason
Array manipulation where collection pipelines are clearer
Using ->first() in situations where exactly one result is expected (should throw if not found)
Missing model casts for booleans, dates, enums, or JSON columns
recommended_pattern: Model relationships + scopes for reusable, readable query composition
Collection pipelines (map/filter/reduce) for readable transforms
Use ->sole() when exactly one record must exist; it throws if 0 or 2+ are found
Use ->firstOrCreate() / ->updateOrCreate() to avoid manual exists-then-insert patterns
Declare $casts on models for booleans, dates, enums, and JSON columns
Enable strict mode in AppServiceProvider (Model::shouldBeStrict()) in non-production to catch lazy loading, missing attributes, and silently discarded fills
Query Builder/raw SQL only when necessary for performance or edge cases

examples.bad: DB::table('orders')->where('token', $token)->first(); // silently returns null if missing
examples.good: Order::where('token', $token)->sole(); // throws ModelNotFoundException or MultipleRecordsFoundException

reference.topic: Prefer to use Eloquent over Query Builder and raw SQL queries

title: No queries in Blade; eager load to avoid N+1
signals: Model::/DB:: queries inside Blade templates
Loops rendering relationships without eager loading
recommended_pattern: Move queries to controller/service layer
Eager load relationships with with()/load()
Use view models/resources to supply already-prepared data

reference.topic: Do not execute queries in Blade templates and use eager loading

title: Chunk/stream large dataset operations
signals: Processing large tables with get() into memory
Long-running loops over many records without chunk/cursor
recommended_pattern: Use chunk(), chunkById(), cursor(), lazy()
Move heavy tasks to queued jobs
Add indexes and reduce selected columns

reference.topic: Chunk data for processing

title: Use Policies for authorization; keep authorize() in the HTTP layer
signals: Inline ability checks (Gate::allows, $user->can, abort(403)) scattered across controllers and services
Authorization logic duplicated across multiple layers or hard-coded role strings
recommended_pattern: Create a Policy class per model with granular ability methods (view, create, update, delete)
Typehint the model in the policy method for automatic model resolution
Call $this->authorize('update', $post) in the controller — never inside service/action classes
Register policies in AuthServiceProvider or rely on automatic policy discovery

examples.bad: if ($request->user()->role !== 'admin') { abort(403); } // inline role check in controller
examples.good: class PostPolicy { public function update(User $user, Post $post): bool { return $user->id === $post->user_id; } }

reference.topic: Authorization using Policies

title: Never call env() outside of config files
signals: env() called directly in controllers, models, services, middleware, or route files
env() used in Blade templates or jobs
recommended_pattern: Declare the env variable in the appropriate config file (config/services.php, config/app.php, etc.)
Access values via config('services.stripe.key') throughout the application
env() should only appear inside config/*.php files

examples.bad: $key = env('STRIPE_KEY'); // in a service class — breaks after config:cache
examples.good: $key = config('services.stripe.key'); // reads from config/services.php which maps to env()

reference.topic: Do not get data from the .env file directly

title: Follow Laravel naming conventions
signals: Controller not using singular PascalCase + Controller suffix
Model not in singular PascalCase; migration not using snake_case describing the change
Method names not in camelCase; config/lang keys not in snake_case
recommended_pattern: Controllers: singular PascalCase + suffix (UserController, not UsersController or user_controller)
Models: singular PascalCase (User, BlogPost — not Users or blog_post)
Migrations: descriptive snake_case (create_posts_table, add_status_to_orders_table)
Methods: camelCase (getUserInfo, not get_user_info)
Routes: kebab-case slugs (/blog-posts, not /blogPosts or /blog_posts)

examples.bad: class users_controller; model name BlogPosts; migration 0001_posts.php
examples.good: class UserController; model BlogPost; migration 2024_01_01_000000_create_blog_posts_table.php

reference.topic: Follow Laravel naming conventions

title: Inertia app: follow the resources/js directory conventions
signals: Mixing generic design-system components with app-specific feature code in the same folders
Unclear boundaries between cross-app components and feature modules
recommended_pattern: Keep shared UI primitives and low-level helpers under resources/js/common
Put domain/feature code under resources/js/modules
Place Inertia-rendered pages in resources/js/pages and keep the structure close to URL paths
Keep shadcn generated components in resources/js/shadcn and wrap/abstract them into common when needed

examples.bad: A project-wide Button component and a posts feature component both living in the same folder
examples.good: resources/js/common/button/Button.tsx for the design primitive; resources/js/modules/posts/components/PostCard.tsx for feature-specific UI

reference.topic: JavaScript application structure for Inertia + React

title: Page components: suffix with 'Page' and default-export the component
signals: Page components named generically (Index.tsx) or exported as named exports
Routing and Inertia lookup failing due to inconsistent default exports
recommended_pattern: Suffix Inertia-rendered components with 'Page' (e.g. PostsIndexPage.tsx)
Use a default export for page components so Inertia can reliably resolve them
Keep only page-level code in page files — helpers/components should live in subdirectories

examples.bad: export function PostsIndex() { ... } // named export and missing Page suffix
examples.good: export default function PostsIndexPage() { ... } // default export and Page suffix

reference.topic: Page component naming and exports

title: Component files: one component per file, PascalCase for components, prefer function declarations
signals: Multiple components declared and exported from a single file
Component file named in camelCase or kebab-case
Components declared as const anonymous arrow functions making stack traces less obvious
recommended_pattern: One component per file, named and exported (named export) using PascalCase
Prefer function declarations (export function Button() { ... }) over const arrow assignments for components
Keep non-component utilities in camelCase filenames, directories in kebab-case

examples.bad: const Button = () => { ... }; const Card = () => { ... }; export { Button, Card };
examples.good: export function Button() { ... } // Button.tsx (single, named export)

reference.topic: Component file organization and naming

title: Treat shadcn-generated components as low-level primitives and wrap them
signals: Direct, widespread imports of shadcn components across the app
Repeated ad-hoc styling or API adaptations in multiple places instead of a single wrapper
recommended_pattern: Keep shadcn components under resources/js/shadcn
Create thin, well-named wrappers in resources/js/common when you need a project-friendly API or default styling
Avoid modifying generated shadcn sources beyond small style adjustments — prefer wrapping so upgrades remain simple

examples.bad: import { Select } from '@/shadcn/select' in many files and adapt props every time
examples.good: Create resources/js/common/components/FruitSelect/FruitSelect.tsx that wraps the shadcn Select and exposes a simple options prop

reference.topic: Managing shadcn/generated components

title: Imports, file casing, and barrel files
signals: Using barrel (index) files to re-export many modules, causing indirection
Inconsistent import paths (relative vs alias) and mixed casing in filenames
recommended_pattern: Avoid barrel files; import components and utilities directly by their absolute aliased path
Use PascalCase for component filenames, camelCase for helper files, and kebab-case for directory names
Keep imports grouped: external libraries first, then application imports; use absolute aliased paths for app imports

examples.bad: import { Button } from '@/common' // ambiguous barrel import
examples.good: import { Button } from '@/common/button/Button' // explicit, single-source import

reference.topic: Import conventions and file casing

title: Type Inertia page props at the component boundary
signals: Page component accepts untyped props or uses 'any' for the props parameter
Accessing usePage().props without a typed generic interface
recommended_pattern: Define a TypeScript interface for each page's props and destructure it in the component parameter
Use usePage<SharedProps>() when reading globally shared data (auth user, flash, etc.)
Keep shared-data types in a central types file (e.g. resources/js/types.ts) and import from there

examples.bad:
export default function PostsIndexPage(props: any) { return
{props.posts}
}
examples.good:
interface PostsIndexPageProps { posts: Post[] }
export default function PostsIndexPage({ posts }: PostsIndexPageProps) { ... }

reference.topic: TypeScript typing for Inertia React page props

title: Use useForm for Inertia form submissions; avoid raw fetch/axios
signals: Form submissions using axios.post, fetch, or jQuery.ajax instead of Inertia's useForm or router
Manual loading/error/redirect state management that Inertia already provides
recommended_pattern: Use useForm({ ...initialValues }) from @inertiajs/react for all form state and submission
Access .processing, .errors, .reset(), and .setData() from the returned form object
Use router.visit(), router.post(), etc. for programmatic (non-form) navigation

examples.bad: axios.post('/posts', { title }).then(res => router.push('/posts')).catch(err => setErrors(err))
examples.good:
const { data, setData, post, processing, errors } = useForm({ title: '' });
function submit(e) { e.preventDefault(); post('/posts'); }

reference.topic: Form handling with useForm in Inertia React

title: Use usePage for shared/global data; avoid prop drilling
signals: Auth user, flash messages, or other server-shared data passed as explicit props through many component layers
Components fetching data that is already available via Inertia shared props
recommended_pattern: Share global data via HandleInertiaRequests::share() on the server side
Access it in any component with usePage<SharedProps>().props without threading props through every layer
Define a SharedProps TypeScript interface centralising the shape of shared data

examples.bad: <Layout user={props.user}><PostsList user={props.user} /></Layout>
examples.good: const { auth } = usePage<SharedProps>().props; // access anywhere without prop drilling

reference.topic: Shared data and usePage in Inertia React

title: Use <Link> from @inertiajs/react for internal navigation; avoid plain <a> tags
signals: Using <a href="..."> for links that navigate within the Inertia app
Full-page reloads triggered by anchor tags that could be client-side visits
recommended_pattern: Use <Link href="/posts"> from @inertiajs/react for all internal navigation
Reserve plain <a> tags for external URLs or file downloads
Use router.visit(), router.get(), router.post() etc. for programmatic navigation

examples.bad: View Posts // triggers a full-page reload
examples.good: <Link href="/posts">View Posts</Link> // Inertia client-side visit

reference.topic: Client-side navigation with the Link component

title: Use partial reloads to refresh only the props you need
signals: Full Inertia page visits used to refresh a single piece of data on the current page
Fetching data with axios/fetch for props that Inertia already provides
recommended_pattern: Use router.reload({ only: ['propName'] }) to refresh a subset of props without a full visit
Mark expensive or non-critical server data as deferred (Inertia::defer) so it loads after the initial render
Avoid mixing Inertia prop refreshes with separate AJAX calls for the same data source

examples.bad: router.visit('/dashboard') // full reload just to refresh a notifications count
examples.good: router.reload({ only: ['notifications'] }) // partial reload, re-fetches only that prop

reference.topic: Partial reloads and deferred props

title: Inertia + Vue app: follow the resources/js directory conventions
signals: Mixing generic design-system components with app-specific feature code in the same folders
Unclear boundaries between cross-app components and feature modules
recommended_pattern: Keep shared UI primitives and low-level helpers under resources/js/common
Put domain/feature code under resources/js/modules
Place Inertia-rendered pages in resources/js/pages and keep the structure close to URL paths
Keep auto-generated or third-party components (e.g. headless UI kit, design-system pieces) in resources/js/lib and wrap/abstract them into common when needed

examples.bad: A project-wide Button component and a posts feature component both living in the same folder
examples.good: resources/js/common/button/Button.vue for the design primitive; resources/js/modules/posts/components/PostCard.vue for feature-specific UI

reference.topic: JavaScript application structure for Inertia + Vue

title: Page components: suffix with 'Page' and default-export the component
signals: Page components named generically (Index.vue) or exported inconsistently
Routing and Inertia lookup failing due to inconsistent export shapes
recommended_pattern: Suffix Inertia-rendered components with 'Page' (e.g. PostsIndexPage.vue)
Use a default export for page components so Inertia can reliably resolve them (using <script setup> or export default)
Keep only page-level code in page files — helpers/components should live in subdirectories

examples.bad: export const PostsIndex = { /* ... */ } // named export and missing Page suffix
examples.good:
<script setup>
// PostsIndexPage.vue
</script>

<!-- default export via script setup compiles to the default component -->
<!-- or: export default defineComponent({ name: 'PostsIndexPage', ... }) -->

reference.topic: Page component naming and exports for Vue

title: Component files: one component per file, PascalCase for components, prefer <script setup>
signals: Multiple components declared and exported from a single file
Component file named in camelCase or kebab-case
Large anonymous options objects making debugging harder
recommended_pattern: One component per .vue file, named using PascalCase (Button.vue)
Prefer <script setup> and composition API for new components; use defineProps/defineEmits for clear typings
Keep non-component utilities as .ts/.js files in camelCase; directories in kebab-case
Group composables in resources/js/common/composables or resources/js/modules/<feature>/composables

examples.bad: // components with multiple related subcomponents in one file
examples.good: resources/js/common/button/Button.vue // single SFC component using <script setup>

reference.topic: Vue component file organization and naming

title: Treat third-party UI library components as low-level primitives and wrap them
signals: Direct, widespread imports of low-level library components across the app
Repeated ad-hoc styling or API adaptations in multiple places instead of a single wrapper
recommended_pattern: Keep third-party or generated components under resources/js/lib or resources/js/vendor
Create thin, well-named wrappers in resources/js/common when you need a project-friendly API or default styling
Avoid modifying upstream library sources — prefer wrapping so upgrades remain simple

examples.bad: import { Dropdown } from 'some-ui-lib' in many files and adapt props every time
examples.good: Create resources/js/common/dropdown/Dropdown.vue that wraps the library and exposes a simpler props API

reference.topic: Managing third-party UI components in Vue apps

title: Imports, file casing, and barrel files (Vue)
signals: Using barrel (index) files to re-export many modules, causing indirection
Inconsistent import paths (relative vs alias) and mixed casing in filenames
recommended_pattern: Avoid barrel files; import components and utilities directly by their absolute aliased path (e.g. @/common/button/Button.vue)
Use PascalCase for component filenames, camelCase for helper files, and kebab-case for directory names
Keep imports grouped: external libraries first, then application imports; prefer alias paths configured in the build tool

examples.bad: import { Button } from '@/common' // ambiguous barrel import
examples.good: import Button from '@/common/button/Button.vue' // explicit, single-source import

reference.topic: Import conventions and file casing for Vue
